english: to get out of bed; to wake up

etymology.description: 起(起) (qǐ, "to rise; to start; to get up from") combined with 床(床) (chuáng, "bed") refers to the action of getting up from a bed, embodying the process of transitioning from a lying position to standing.
etymology.interpretation: 起(起) (qǐ, "to rise") conveys the action of moving upward or beginning an action. In this context, it refers explicitly to starting the day by getting up. When paired with 床(床) (chuáng, "bed"), it specifies the exact place from which one rises, thus directly invoking the act of getting out of bed. The sense of 'to wake up' derives from this physical action as waking is the mental state accompanying rising.

parts.english: to rise; to start; to get up from

examples.chinese: 我每天早上六点起床(我每天早上六點起床)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ měitiān zǎoshang liù diǎn qǐ chuáng.
examples.english: I wake up at six every morning.

examples.chinese: 天气冷时(天氣冷時)，我不想起床(我不想起床)。
examples.pinyin: Tiānqì lěng shí, wǒ bù xiǎng qǐ chuáng.
examples.english: When the weather is cold, I don't want to get out of bed.

examples.chinese: 他迟到了因为起床太晚(他遲到了因為起床太晚)。
examples.pinyin: Tā chídào le yīnwèi qǐ chuáng tài wǎn.
examples.english: He was late because he got out of bed too late.
